subject: Accounting
title: Whistleblowers Who Changed History
description: A 60-second lesson on Whistleblowers Who Changed History.

hook.text: Cynthia Cooper worked nights at WorldCom, digging through records. She found $3.8 billion in fraud.

buildup.text: Whistleblowers—insiders who report wrongdoing—are behind many of the biggest fraud discoveries.

discovery.text: Sherron Watkins warned Enron's CEO. Cooper alerted WorldCom's board. Both were ignored—at first.

twist.text: Before legal protections, whistleblowers were routinely fired, blacklisted, and destroyed.

climax.text: Sarbanes-Oxley and Dodd-Frank now protect and even reward whistleblowers financially.

punchline.text: The bravest accountants don't just count. They speak up.
